findings.fix: delete the abbrev or make it a local notation; export under the canonical name
findings.issue: `abbrev baseChange := K ⊗[k] A` duplicates the tensor-product type already carrying the Hopf algebra instance (Mathlib/RingTheory/HopfAlgebra/TensorProduct.lean:41)

findings.fix: use/alias the Mathlib `Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map` directly or request an upstream export
findings.issue: `map` is exactly `Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map (BialgHom.id K K) f` (already in Bialgebra.TensorProduct); the new simp lemmas `map_*` and `map_comp` are reproofs of the existing general lemmas

findings.evidence: PR lines 88,100,110; Mathlib algebra tensor product includeRight definition
findings.fix: re-export the existing declarations instead of redeclaring with new names
findings.issue: `includeRight` and the Coalgebra/Hopf simp lemmas are direct renamings of `Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight` plus the already-proved tensor-product actions

summary: The PR adds a parallel named API (`baseChange`, `includeRight`, `map`, pointwise simp lemmas) that directly re-wraps existing `K ⊗[k] A`, `Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight`, and `Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map` from Mathlib.RingTheory.HopfAlgebra.TensorProduct and Bialgebra.TensorProduct; every new declaration is a special case or renaming of material already provided there.
